 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-2vbk","title":"[IDEA-10] Event Threading Normalization Utilities","description":"# Event Threading Normalization Utilities

## Goal
Provide a normalized thread/topic representation across connectors (Telegram forums, Slack threads, Discord threads) to simplify downstream agent logic.

## Background / Why
Thread semantics differ across platforms; a unified schema reduces connector-specific branching and improves agent portability.

## Scope
- Add optional `thread` metadata to EventEnvelope and/or a helper struct.
- Provide helper to map platform-specific thread ids into normalized fields.

## Deliverables
- `ThreadInfo { thread_id, parent_id, kind }` helper + docs.
- Example mappings for Telegram forum topics and Discord/Slack threads.

## Tests
- Unit tests for mapping functions.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Agents can rely on normalized thread metadata without platform-specific parsing.","notes":"[AlienArtifactPractical]
- Add canonicalization proof tests: permute fields -> canonical bytes identical.
- Add deterministic vector generator with fixed seeds and algebraic edge cases.
[ExtremeOptimizationPractical]
- Batch ed25519 verification and reuse hashers where safe.
- Avoid allocations in canonicalization using stack buffers.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-2vdwq","title":"[FCP3] Mesh first refoundation from host first truthful surface to a V3 native platform","description":"Background: the current repository is truthful but host first in day to day operation. The practical control path runs through crates/fwc/src/main.rs, crates/fwc/src/catalog.rs, crates/fwc/src/readiness.rs, and crates/fcp-host/src/bin/fcp-host.rs, while crates/fcp-core/src/lib.rs still behaves like an overly broad semantic bucket and crates/fcp-protocol/src/lib.rs does not yet own the full FCPC and FCPS story.

Goal: make the platform genuinely mesh first in the sense intended by the V3 architecture, not merely more mesh flavored in naming or CLI copy. That means moving execution semantics, policy, evidence, durable truth, placement, leases, and failover into first class owners so that fcp-host becomes a node local root app over mesh native substrate layers and fwc can stay truthful while projecting mesh backed reality.

This initiative is the canonical migration graph. Success means: each major semantic noun has exactly one owner; FCPC is the normative live control and evidence plane; FCPS is the normative durable object plane; host local process state stops being the only source of truth for receipts, checkpoints, leases, and readiness; fwc consumes stable contracts instead of semantic buckets; and the cutover ends with deletion of transition scaffolding rather than permanent compatibility debt.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"initiative","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:00.136601Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","updated_at":"2026-04-26T00:29:49.171173Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T00:29:49.170484Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["architecture","fcp3","fcp3-refoundation","initiative","mesh-first"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-2vdwq","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-kipeo","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:17.633269Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-2vdwq","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-nw3xn","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:18.198810Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-2vdwq","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-q6fx6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:17.932261Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-2vdwq","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-rnphw","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:17.323051Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-2vdwq","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-smptq","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:18.895972Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-2vdwq","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-t1hy0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:19.195276Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-2vdwq","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-v2uw9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:18.612739Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2047,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-2vdwq","author":"CobaltHarbor","text":"This initiative exists to prevent a common failure mode: making the CLI or docs sound more mesh native before the runtime, durability, evidence, and placement layers actually are. If a future implementer has to choose between appearing more mesh first and staying strictly truthful about what the platform can prove, truthful provenance wins.

Use this graph as the self contained replacement for the spec derived migration narrative. The intended order is: freeze semantics, carve real owners, realign durable and wire truth, converge host and SDK, prove mesh placement and failover on a narrow pilot, move operator surfaces onto those contracts, then delete the transition scaffolding. No compatibility shims, no permanent host local truth islands, and no fake mesh claims.","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:00Z"},{"id":2055,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-2vdwq","author":"CobaltHarbor","text":"Graph hygiene note from creation pass on 2026-03-27: br dep cycles still reports 22 pre-existing cycles in older unrelated beads, but none of the new FCP3 initiative IDs created in this pass appear in that cycle set. This mesh-first initiative was added as an acyclic subgraph with one root initiative, seven phase epics, and thirty five task beads.","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:33:39Z"},{"id":2057,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-2vdwq","author":"CobaltHarbor","text":"Plan space revision on 2026-03-27: added the dedicated label fcp3-refoundation so bv analysis can isolate this initiative from older unrelated FCP3 beads. Also split the original P1.1 bottleneck into three parallel slice inventories plus one synthesis bead, and tightened the proof beads so unit tests, end to end scripts, structured logging, and artifact capture are explicit requirements rather than implied expectations.","created_at":"2026-03-27T20:22:44Z"},{"id":2066,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-2vdwq","author":"jemanuel","text":"Plan-space polish round on 2026-03-27: tightened Phase 7 so deletion can no longer outrun proof, added an explicit performance/resource proof bead for cutover-critical flows, made the transition scorecard guardrail-aware, and sharpened P4/P6 beads so connector-author UX, machine-readable CLI contracts, operator remediation, structured diagnostics, and reproducible logging are first-class requirements instead of implied nice-to-haves.","created_at":"2026-03-27T20:30:23Z"},{"id":2068,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-2vdwq","author":"jemanuel","text":"2026-03-28 polish round: added a dedicated pre-cutover baseline bead so performance and UX proof compare against preserved user-visible reality instead of memory after phase 7 deletions. Also tightened final review to wait on earlier phase closeout, and made docs/transcripts explicitly derive from acceptance and e2e artifacts so operator teaching stays honest.","created_at":"2026-03-28T05:14:13Z"},{"id":2071,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-2vdwq","author":"jemanuel","text":"2026-03-28 verification-hand-off polish: tightened the foundational semantic and runtime beads so they explicitly call for deterministic fixtures, transcripts, audit or trace fields, and compile-time or replayable evidence that later preservation, conformance, integration, and e2e beads will consume. This reduces the risk that verification remains implied instead of mechanically grounded.","created_at":"2026-03-28T05:38:32Z"},{"id":2889,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-2vdwq","author":"jemanuel","text":"All 7 phase epics closed: P1 rnphw (semantic lock), P2 kipeo (kernel/policy/evidence carve), P3 q6fx6 (FCPC/FCPS wire), P4 nw3xn (host+SDK convergence), P5 v2uw9 (placement/leases/failover), P6 smptq (CLI/operator truth), P7 t1hy0 (cutover + proof). FCP3 mesh-first refoundation complete with all proof artifacts published. Closing initiative.","created_at":"2026-04-26T00:29:48Z"}]}

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-2vqb","title":"[IDEA-11.2] Streaming health log fields","description":"## Goal
Standardize structured log fields for streaming health events.

## Details
- Add log schema fields: `heartbeat_seq`, `ack_seq`, `missed_heartbeats`, `reconnect_count`.
- Ensure logs are JSONL-compatible and do not leak secrets.

## Tests
- Log format tests (schema validation depends on flywheel_connectors-11g2).

## Acceptance Criteria
- Health-related logs are consistent across connectors.","notes":"Added streaming health log fields + counters in fcp-sdk runtime

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-2vvy","title":"[FCP2] InvokeRequest/InvokeResponse: Schema Validation, Holder Proof, Binding Rules","description":"# [FCP2] InvokeRequest/InvokeResponse: Schema Validation, Holder Proof, Binding Rules

## Goal
Implement the canonical InvokeRequest and InvokeResponse structures that are the primary interface for connector invocation.

## Why This Deserves Its Own Bead
The spec defines detailed requirements for InvokeRequest/InvokeResponse that span multiple concerns:
- capability token embedding and holder_proof generation
- idempotency_key handling
- lease binding (when required)
- evidence attachment rules
- response envelope structure

This complexity warrants focused implementation and testing.

## InvokeRequest (NORMATIVE)
Fields:
- `request_id` (UUID)
- `connector_id` (ConnectorId)
- `operation_id` (SchemaId)
- `zone_id` (ZoneId)
- `token` (CapabilityToken - COSE_Sign1 bytes)
- `holder_proof` (Ed25519 signature; REQUIRED when token.holder_node present)
- `idempotency_key` (Option<String>) - REQUIRED for Risky/Dangerous ops
- `inputs` (CBOR map - schema-validated)
- `context` (InvokeContext - trace_id, request_tags, etc.)
- `lease_seq` (Option<u64>) - REQUIRED for singleton_writer connectors

### holder_proof Binding (NORMATIVE)
When token has `holder_node` set:
- signable bytes MUST bind: `request_id || operation_id || token.jti`
- signature by holder node signing key
- prevents replay by non-holder nodes

### Idempotency Rules
- For `IdempotencyPolicy::Strict` or Risky/Dangerous: idempotency_key MUST be set
- Key format: opaque string, max 128 bytes

## InvokeResponse (NORMATIVE)
Fields:
- `request_id` (matches request)
- `status` (Ok | Error)
- `outputs` (Option<CBOR map> - schema-validated)
- `error` (Option<FcpError>)
- `receipt_id` (Option<ObjectId> - OperationReceipt)
- `audit_event_id` (Option<ObjectId>)
- `decision_receipt_id` (Option<ObjectId> - for denials)
- `response_metadata` (timing, cache hints, etc.)

## Subtasks
- Define InvokeRequest and InvokeResponse canonical schemas
- Implement holder_proof generation and verification
- Implement idempotency_key validation
- Implement lease_seq binding checks
- Define InvokeContext propagation rules

## Acceptance Criteria
- Golden vectors for InvokeRequest canonical encoding
- Adversarial tests: missing holder_proof rejected, invalid idempotency_key rejected
- Property tests: holder_proof binding covers all required fields

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-jdef7","title":"[REVIEW-A9-FUZZ][P2] fcp-host: missing fuzz target for InvokeRequest dispatch chain (verify_unbound + zone-policy)","description":"fuzz/fuzz_targets/host_admin_api.rs covers HostSimulateRequest deserialization, but the higher-value attack surface is the InvokeRequest dispatch chain on /rpc/invoke:

  1. serde_json::from_slice::<InvokeRequest>(body) — the wire decoder
  2. CapabilityVerifier::verify_unbound(token, cap, op, resource_uris)
     - cose verify + claim parse + nbf/exp + zone binding
  3. simulate_policy_decision(...) — zone policy + transport + posture
  4. CancellationController::track_with_owner(op_id, principal)
  5. connector dispatch

Steps 1-3 are deterministic, pure Rust, take attacker-controlled bytes, and have NO end-to-end fuzz target. The existing capability.rs and host_admin_api.rs targets cover pieces in isolation; nothing exercises the COMPOSITION the way the live invoke handler does.

fuzzable input shape:

#[derive(Arbitrary, Debug)]
struct InvokeFuzzInput {
    body_bytes: Vec<u8>,        // attacker-controlled JSON wire body
    asserted_principal: Option<String>,  // X-Principal header
    zone_policy_seed: u64,      // pick from a small pre-built ZonePolicyObject set
}

fuzz_target!(|input: InvokeFuzzInput| {
    // Build a deterministic verifier + state, decode body, verify token,
    // simulate policy decision. No connector dispatch — we only fuzz the
    // pre-execution gate that decides allow/deny.
    let _ = invoke_dispatch_dry_run(&input.body_bytes, input.asserted_principal.as_deref(), input.zone_policy_seed);
});

properties to exercise:
- decode that yields an InvokeRequest with attacker-controlled capability_token bytes must not bypass verify_unbound (already pinned in tests, but fuzz amplifies coverage)
- zone_id mismatch between request and capability claim must always reject (cross-zone escape)
- principal mismatch between asserted_principal and capability subject must reject (br-jdaro regression)
- claims with absurd nbf/exp timestamps must surface TokenNotYetValid / TokenExpired, not panic on saturating arithmetic
- empty input / 1-byte input / multi-MB input must all return Result with bounded work

priority rationale: P2. invoke is the single hottest production code path. Composition bugs across the verify_unbound + zone-policy + cancellation chain are the class of defect ohpg7, jdaro, and 0k3d4 surfaced; a fuzzer exercising the whole chain would catch the next one before code review does.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-27T02:21:22.006330Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-27T02:21:22.006330Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-jgtlf","title":"[REVIEW-A9][MEDIUM] fcp-sandbox/wasi.rs:1261: missing writable preopens widen file grants into directory authority","description":"prepare_preopened_dir() rejects existing file preopens but auto-creates any missing writable path with create_dir_all(path) and mounts it as a directory. Repro: declare a missing writable path like /tmp/fcp-secret.json, call WasiRuntime::new(...).create_store(), and the guest receives writable directory authority under /tmp/fcp-secret.json/. Manifest sandbox path validation also allows ambiguous absolute file-like strings, so this bypasses the existing file-only guard. Fix: fail closed on missing writable preopens unless the schema distinguishes directories from file grants.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T23:25:33.403054Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-26T23:51:31.163547Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T23:51:31.163297Z","close_reason":"Fixed in 9848c419: reject implicit writable preopen auto-directory grants","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-k1bwk","title":"fuzz: fcp-cbor SchemaId hash injectivity (collision space)","description":"Add fuzz_schema_id_hash target. Property: for arbitrary (namespace, name, version) tuples, equal tuples ⟹ equal hashes; distinct tuples ⟹ distinct hashes. Current unit tests cover hand-picked separator-collision pairs (post-mzi9x SchemaId::hash separator-collision fix at SchemaId::feed_length_prefixed); fuzz extends to arbitrary collision-search space. Also probes try_new validation: ':' and '@' in namespace/name must be rejected.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-27T19:15:02.021191Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-27T19:24:50.591111Z","closed_at":"2026-04-27T19:24:50.590649Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fuzz","tests"],"comments":[{"id":2969,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-k1bwk","author":"jemanuel","text":"Added fuzz_schema_id_hash target. Asserts: (1) determinism — re-hashing same SchemaId yields identical digest, (2) injectivity — distinct (namespace,name,version) tuples ⟹ distinct 32-byte hashes (probes the post-mzi9x length-prefixed encoding's collision resistance, including reserved-separator boundary attacks via direct field construction that bypasses try_new), (3) validation parity — try_new accepts iff no ':' or '@' in components, and accepted SchemaIds hash identically to direct construction. Compiles + clippy clean via rch (with local fallback when worker toolchain drifts).","created_at":"2026-04-27T19:24:50Z"}]}

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-mrln7","title":"[AUDIT-mock-code-finder][MEDIUM] fwc access attach_bundle ignores storage lookup","description":"crates/fwc/src/access_cmd.rs:1139 attach_bundle() comment: 'In a real implementation, this would look up the bundle from storage.  {"id":"flywheel_connectors-mrnbf","title":"[REVIEW-A9][P1] crates/fcp-registry/src/lib.rs:530: public verifier result structs let callers forge supply-chain evidence","description":"SupplyChainEvidence keeps tuf_verified/sigstore_verified private, but its public promotion methods accept TufVerificationResult and SigstoreVerificationResult. Those result structs have public fields at crates/fcp-registry/src/lib.rs:530 and :541, so any downstream caller can construct TufVerificationResult { verified: true, .. } or SigstoreVerificationResult { verified: true, .. } without invoking the verifier traits, then pass it to with_tuf_verification_result / with_sigstore_verification_result and satisfy require_tuf / require_sigstore. Transparency evidence has the same shape via public SupplyChainEvidence::transparency_log_present and with_transparency_log_present. Fix should make verifier success evidence non-forgeable across crate boundaries (for example sealed/private proof token or move verification+promotion behind registry-owned adapter calls) and add external-style regression coverage proving forged results/evidence cannot satisfy SupplyChainVerificationConfig.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T22:35:25.716337Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-26T23:44:05.192842Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T23:44:05.191909Z","close_reason":"Fixed in 773e3dd1: sealed supply-chain verifier success evidence against downstream forgery","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

- Attempt enrollment without posture; deny with reason code.

- JSONL entries for enrollment outcomes.

- Persist JSONL artifacts and validate with `fcp_conformance::schemas::validate_e2e_log_jsonl`.

- Avoid allocations in canonicalization using stack buffers.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-n4429","title":"[REVIEW-A9][P1] crates/fwc/src/main.rs:15036: registry signature verification trusts downloaded key","description":"prepare_registry_package_artifact parses the Ed25519 verifying key directly from the registry-downloaded ManifestSignatureArtifact, then inserts that same key into RegistryTrustPolicy before calling RegistryVerifier::verify_bundle. A malicious registry endpoint, or a MITM when --registry uses http://, can serve a manifest/binary/signature artifact signed by an attacker-controlled key and include that public key in the artifact, causing the registry-signature check to pass without any configured trust root or pinned publisher key. Fix by anchoring registry publisher keys in local/trusted configuration or TUF/Sigstore roots and rejecting artifacts whose key_id/verifying_key is not pre-authorized; also reject non-local plain HTTP registry endpoints or make them explicit test/dev-only.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T23:11:56.219996Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-26T23:42:02.005230Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T23:42:02.004735Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":2917,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-n4429","author":"jemanuel","text":"Fixed in commit f17bf191 (cc_2/SunnyMoose). prepare_registry_package_artifact now anchors verification on LOCAL pinned trust roots (FCP_REGISTRY_TRUST_ROOTS_FILE env var, or test-only thread-local override) instead of TOFU on the registry-supplied verifying_key. Two-layer defense: (1) signature_artifact.key_id MUST be in pinned set or refuse; (2) artifact verifying_key bytes MUST match pinned bytes (catches kid-collision-with-bad-bytes). Pinned key (not artifact bytes) is used for both attestation verification and RegistryVerifier::verify_bundle. 2 regression tests: install_registry_rejects_unpinned_publisher_key + install_registry_rejects_pinned_kid_with_mismatched_key_bytes. All 7 install_registry tests passing. Operator action: existing deployments must ship FCP_REGISTRY_TRUST_ROOTS_FILE JSON before registry installs work.","created_at":"2026-04-26T23:42:01Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-n6r","title":"fcp.terraform: Safety-critical design enforcement (Plan=read-only, Apply=approval)","description":"# fcp.terraform: Safety-critical design enforcement (Plan=read-only, Apply=approval) (FCP2)

## Goal
Make Terraform operations mechanically safe:
- plan is always read-only
- apply is always approval-gated and bound to a specific plan hash
- destroy is forbidden by default

This bead defines the safety envelope that all other terraform operations must obey.

## External surface (NetworkConstraints)
Default deny.
- Network egress MUST be restricted per-operation via manifest `NetworkConstraints`.
- Allow only the configured service host(s); HTTPS-only where applicable; deny redirects by default.
- Bound timeouts + response sizes; treat all remote responses as untrusted input; never leak secrets/PII in logs or errors.

## Core safety rules (non-negotiable)
- Never run Terraform with `-auto-approve`.
- Always run with `-input=false`.
- Apply requires:
  - a previously generated plan reference
  - a matching plan hash
  - an ApprovalToken when policy requires
- Explicit denial reason codes for:
  - missing plan
  - hash mismatch
  - drift detected
  - destroy forbidden

## Capability model (illustrative)
- `terraform.plan`
- `terraform.apply` (dangerous; approval required)
- `terraform.destroy` (critical; disabled unless explicitly enabled)

## FCP2 requirements
- Single-zone binding.
- Sandbox profile must:
  - restrict filesystem to workspace allowlist
  - restrict process execution to terraform binary
- Audit:
  - all state reads are audited
  - all applies produce receipts + audit events

## Deliverables
- Safety gate layer used by all terraform operations.
- Canonical DecisionReceipt reason codes for denials.

## Test strategy (MUST)
- Unit tests for:
  - refusal of auto-approve
  - refusal of apply without plan
  - hash mismatch denial
  - destroy forbidden default
  - audit event emission on state reads

## Acceptance criteria
- It is mechanically impossible to apply/destroy without explicit authorization.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-n8tr","title":"[IDEA-22.1] Implement posture attestation verification + policy hooks","description":"## What
Add posture attestation schema + verification, and integrate with policy evaluation.

## How
- Define schema + validation rules in `fcp-core`/`fcp-conformance`.
- Extend policy engine to enforce `requires_posture` in ZonePolicy.
- Emit DecisionReceipts with evidence on denial.

## Success Criteria
- Verification rejects malformed or expired posture attestations.

## Acceptance Criteria (Refined)
- Invalid signature or expired attestation is rejected.
- Evidence ObjectIds are recorded in DecisionReceipt.
","notes":"Implemented posture attestation verification and policy hooks. Added PostureAttestation, PostureRequirements, PostureAttributeKey types. Extended ZonePolicyObject with requires_posture field. Added DecisionReasonCodes for posture failures. Integrated with PolicyEngine.evaluate_invoke().

[AlienArtifactPractical]
- Add evidence ledger fields with per-check log-odds contributions and explicit loss decision.
- Add anytime-valid risk control (e-process) for approvals/denials and record epsilon in receipts.
[ExtremeOptimizationPractical]
- Compile policies to DAG/BDD and cache allow/deny results with LRU.
- Use bitset capability sets for fast subset checks.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-zcmu.2","title":"fcp.snowflake: Provisioning Automation","description":"## Goal
Automate provisioning and configuration with minimal human steps.

This bead owns:
- auth setup (OAuth / API keys / webhook secrets) via `CredentialObject`/`CredentialId` (egress proxy injection preferred)
- endpoint configuration
- `fcp doctor` readiness checks
- deterministic, mockable provisioning flows

## FCP2 requirements
- Secrets never touch disk.
- Provisioning is deterministic and testable.
- Error messages are actionable and do not leak secrets/PII.

## Acceptance Criteria
- No real external calls in unit tests.
- Provisioning emits structured logs per `flywheel_connectors-1n78.35`.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-zcmu.3","title":"TEST: fcp.snowflake Unit/Integration Tests (mock-only)","description":"## Goal
Mock-only unit/integration tests for this connector.

## Required coverage
- schema validation (inputs + outputs)
- error taxonomy mapping (retry semantics)
- pagination and bounds
- rate limit behavior
- redaction (no token/secret/PII leakage)
- idempotency + receipts for Dangerous operations

## Logging
All tests emit structured JSON logs per `flywheel_connectors-1n78.35`.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Deterministic tests; no live external calls.
- Includes adversarial cases (timeouts, malformed responses, partial failures).

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-zcmu.4","title":"E2E: fcp.snowflake Connector Compliance Run","description":"## Goal
Run the shared connector compliance runner and capture an evidence bundle.

## Must validate
- handshake + introspection correctness
- default deny (no CapabilityToken → deny + DecisionReceipt)

- receipts/audit for Dangerous operations
- structured logs

## Acceptance Criteria
- E2E run is deterministic and produces a stable artifact bundle.

+{"id":"flywheel_connectors-zcxit","title":"TEST-FUZZ-ED25519: pubkey/sig parser + non-malleability under verify_strict","description":"Add fuzz_ed25519_verify target. Properties: (1) Ed25519VerifyingKey::from_bytes is panic-free across all 32-byte inputs and rejects all-zero + small-subgroup points; (2) sign(msg)→verify(msg,sig) succeeds; (3) wrong-key verify rejects; (4) bit-mutated signature OR message MUST NOT verify (verify_strict non-malleability — guards against the historical Ed25519 malleable-S attack documented at ed25519.rs:204+). No existing target covers ed25519 directly; cose_roundtrip exercises COSE-wrapped signatures only.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-27T19:27:16.074792Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-27T19:27:19.221889Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fuzz","tests"]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-zkj2d","title":"[REVIEW][MEDIUM] fcp-registry/lib.rs: signed package symlinked binary bypasses standalone-file check","description":"file: crates/fcp-registry/src/lib.rs:2100-2149

repro:
1. Create a signed package directory with a valid manifest/signature and a real binary file inside the package directory.
2. Replace the declared signature.binary_name path with a symlink inside the package directory that points to that real sibling file.
3. Load the package through LocalRegistryCatalog::from_signed_package_dirs().
4. The loader canonicalizes the path and then uses std::fs::metadata() on the resolved target, so the symlink passes as a regular file as long as it stays inside the package directory.
5. That contradicts the LinkedBinary contract that the binary must be a standalone regular file.

suggested fix:
Use symlink_metadata() on the originally joined binary path and reject symlinks before following them, then keep the existing traversal and hard-link checks.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T01:04:14.002416Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-26T01:15:30.421993Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T01:15:30.421669Z","close_reason":"Rejected symlinked signed package binaries and added regression coverage","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-zp1xx","title":"[REVIEW-P4][MEDIUM] fcp-crypto::CwtClaims::constraints_cbor panics on invalid CBOR","description":"crates/fcp-crypto/src/cose.rs:223-235 documents that constraints_cbor takes pre-serialized CBOR, but it deserializes with ciborium::from_reader(cbor_bytes).expect("constraints_cbor: invalid CBOR"). Bug scenario: any caller that forwards malformed or attacker-supplied constraint bytes into this public builder path can abort the process instead of getting a typed CryptoError. This is especially dangerous because the rest of the COSE/CWT surface is fail-closed and error-returning, so a single malformed constraints blob becomes a panic denial-of-service instead of a normal token-construction failure. Suggested fix: make constraints_cbor return CryptoResult<Self> (or add a fallible companion and deprecate the panicking variant) and reject invalid CBOR without unwinding.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T20:33:00.235836Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T20:41:22.434941Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T20:41:22.432735Z","close_reason":"Added fallible CwtClaims::try_constraints_cbor + CapabilityTokenBuilder::try_constraints_cbor returning CryptoResult<Self> with CryptoError::SerializationError on invalid CBOR. The existing panic-prone constraints_cbor is now #[deprecated] AND no longer panics — it tracing-warns and leaves the constraints claim UNSET on malformed input, so a legacy caller's worst-case outcome is a token without constraints that the verifier's C3.4 constraints-required gate rejects at verify time, strictly better than a process abort. Added tracing as a production dep of fcp-crypto. 4 new regression tests (malformed CBOR rejection via try_ variant, valid CBOR acceptance, builder wrapper propagation, legacy shim no-panic behavior). Verified: 554 fcp-crypto lib tests pass (was 550 + 4 new).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
